prompt: Review the recently added bounds checks in bounded_slice_copy and repair the implementation without changing its public API. The function returns EINVAL for a null destination, zero destination capacity, a null source, or a negative source offset. Whenever the destination is writable, every failure must leave it as an empty string. Return ERANGE when the requested count exceeds BOUNDED_SLICE_MAX, the source slice is outside source_length, or the destination cannot hold the copied bytes plus the terminating NUL. Source-range arithmetic must not wrap or rely on a signed-to-unsigned conversion. A slice of exactly BOUNDED_SLICE_MAX bytes is valid when the source contains it and the destination has BOUNDED_SLICE_MAX + 1 bytes. Preserve successful empty-slice behavior, keep `make clean test` offline, and do not modify `.gitignore`, `Makefile`, `include/bounded_slice.h`, or the tests.
